sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed! Clicking on the white cell at (50,30) changed it to green (N). I can see in the image that the top-center cell in the grid is now green instead of white. So clicking on cells in the grid changes their color. Let me understand the pattern - I need to figure out what the target pattern is. The left panel seems to be the "target" pattern with purple dots. Let me check the current grid state and figure out the mapping.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The white cell at (49,29) changed to green! Let me check the current grid state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current grid cells (lower right):")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] >= 7 and n['id'] <= 22:
        # Get center of the cell
        bnd = n['boundary']
        r = sum(p[0] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        c = sum(p[1] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({r},{c})")

# Let me also check the left panel pattern
print("\nLeft panel pattern:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] >= 11 and n['id'] <= 19:
        bnd = n['boundary']
        r = sum(p[0] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        c = sum(p[1] for p in bnd) // len(bnd)
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({r},{c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

**Left panel (target pattern)**:

- The background is gray (g)
**Right grid (current state)**:
Row 1: gray(8) - GREEN(9) - gray(10)
Row 2: white(16) - gray(17) - white(18)
Row 3: gray(20) - white(21) - gray(22)
The left panel has a cross pattern with purple dots. The right grid needs to match this pattern. The purple dots mark which cells should be changed.
Looking at the left panel more carefully:
- It's a 9x9 panel (rows 50-59, cols 11-20)
- The 3x3 grid cells would be at:
  - (51-53, 12-14), (51-53, 15-17), (51-53, 18-20)
  - (54-56, 12-14), (54-56, 15-17), (54-56, 18-20)
  - (57-59, 12-14), (57-59, 15-17), (57-59, 18-20)
Purple dots at:
- (51,15) → top-center cell
- (54,12) → middle-left cell
- (54,18) → middle-right cell
- (57,15) → bottom-center cell
So the target pattern is a cross/plus: the 4 cardinal directions should be changed to green (or some target color).
The right grid currently has:
- Top row: gray, GREEN (changed!), gray
- Middle row: white, gray, white
- Bottom row: gray, white, gray
I already changed the top-center to green. I need to change:
- Middle-left (55,25) - currently white → needs to become green
- Middle-right (55,35) - currently white → needs to become green
- Bottom-center (60,30) - currently white → needs to become green
Wait, but I need to understand the color cycling. When I clicked white, it became green (N). Let me check if clicking again cycles through colors or if there's a specific target.
Actually, looking at the left panel, the purple dots are ON TOP of the gray background. The target pattern shows which cells should be "active" or changed. The right grid needs to have the same cross pattern.
